articles.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
articles.summary: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

articles.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Declares Strict Control of Vital Shipping Route
articles.summary: The day after Iran declared the vital waterway open, it reversed course, injecting new peril into navigation there.

articles.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Colonialism
articles.summary: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.

articles.title: Pope Says News Outlets Misread Some of His Remarks as Criticism of Trump
articles.summary: Pope Leo responded directly on Monday to a presidential attack. But since then, he said, some of his statements during his Africa trip have been misconstrued.

articles.title: ‘Turning Point Was Charlie Kirk’: Why This Student Group Moved On
articles.summary: Students at the University of Arkansas disagreed with Turning Point’s direction, pointing to challenges ahead for the conservative group.

articles.title: The Doctor Will Seek Your Vote Now
articles.summary: Dozens of Democratic doctors are running for office in the midterms, including some spurred by opposition to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his anti-vaccine stance.

articles.title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz Would Ease Oil Crisis but Only So Much
articles.summary: Analysts said energy and shipping companies would be reluctant to fully restore operations until they were confident that hostilities were over.

articles.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
articles.summary: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contes

articles.title: Opinion: Remembering Kevin Klose, former NPR president and broadcasting icon
articles.summary: We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.

articles.title: Ukraine launches specialized Russian studies program to better understand the enemy
articles.summary: Ukraine launches political Russian studies program to better understand wartime enemy.

articles.title: Maduro’s Successor Is Purging Allies Who Kept Him in Power in Venezuela
articles.summary: The successor to Venezuela’s captured President Nicolás Maduro is purging the people who kept him in power.

articles.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
articles.summary: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

articles.title: She invited her friends to come together to make her casket
articles.summary: Friends gathered at a weaver's studio in Massachusetts to help MaddyChristine Hope Brokopp make her casket.

articles.title: Caracas' iconic macaws threatened by vanishing palm trees
articles.summary: In the Venezuelan capital, Caracas, flocks of colorful macaws that once brightened city skies now face disappearing nest sites — and with them, a unique urban bond.

articles.title: Iran says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, as ceasefire nears its end
articles.summary: Iran's military said on Saturday the Strait of Hormuz has "returned to its previous state." The announcement came after President Trump had said the blockade on Iranian ports would remain in place.

articles.title: Primary Becomes Purity Test for a State G.O.P.: ‘You Can’t Serve Two Masters’
articles.summary: For years, Republican state legislators in Montana have been willing to team up with Democrats, but in nearly two dozen races on June 2, a nationally attuned right has those lawmakers in its sights.

articles.title: What's it like to negotiate with Iran? We asked people who have done it
articles.summary: Experts who spent months negotiating a 2015 nuclear agreement with Iran say mutual mistrust, starkly different negotiating styles, and the complexity of the issues make a quick deal unlikely.

articles.title: Animal activists celebrate their first global 'Sanctuary Day'
articles.summary: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
